response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems software developer with genuine performance engineering experience (high-throughput micro-services, async I/O, low-latency pipelines), but the Principal Performance Architect role demands highly specialized expertise in SoC and IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, and deep-learning workload optimization at the silicon level — none of which appear anywhere in his background. His recent AI-adjacent work at re:cinq is limited to MCP tooling and agentic workflow infrastructure, not hardware-level AI accelerator analysis. The skills gap between a senior web/systems software generalist and a hardware-software co-design principal at this level is too wide to bridge at interview.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software engineering with demonstrated performance-conscious design (async I/O, caching, fiber-based concurrency, high-throughput micro-services at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second)
Low-level systems programming experience in C, C++, and DLang, including embedded and robotic-arm work, showing comfort below the application layer
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflow tooling at re:cinq, indicating awareness of the AI space
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience; the role's core technical requirement is entirely absent from his resume
No GPU, SIMD, or MIMD architecture background and no deep neural network training or inference optimization work of any kind
Career trajectory is firmly in web, full-stack, and DevOps software engineering — no hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator analysis, or silicon-level performance work

key_factors.factor: Absent SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU optimization background
Performance-oriented software engineering in high-throughput systems
